title: Fetch-to-Commit Ingestion Unit, Checkpoints, and Partial Failure · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs fetch-to-commit ingestion unit, checkpoints, and partial failure from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for atomic feed ingestion unit of work after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Model claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, normalized, committing, committed, failed, canceled, and superseded states.
Commit feed metadata, entries, revisions, validators, next schedule, and audit record together or preserve the prior admitted version.
Checkpoint only replay-safe boundaries and bind each checkpoint to source, validator, parser version, schema, and attempt identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Feed Go Ingestion Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 2811 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile fetch, parse, normalize, atomic commit, validators, checkpoints, and post-commit effects; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Reconcile fetched, admitted, inserted, updated, unchanged, rejected, quarantined, and committed counts exactly.

Inspect the Feed Go Ingestion Reconciliation trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Ingestion Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Ingestion Reconciliation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 5144 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile fetch, parse, normalize, atomic commit, validators, checkpoints, and post-commit effects; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Reconcile fetched, admitted, inserted, updated, unchanged, rejected, quarantined, and committed counts exactly.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Ingestion Reconciliation.
